description: Editing a config file value that is overridden at runtime by an environment variable, so the change has no effect.
body: Failure mode: a setting is changed in a config file while an environment variable for the same key takes precedence, so the running process keeps the old value and the fix appears to do nothing. Detection signals: the file shows the new value but the process behaves with the old one; the env var is set in the shell/container; restart does not change behavior. Do not edit a layer that is shadowed by a higher-precedence source. Safer fallback: read the effective resolved value first, identify the winning layer, change that layer, and confirm by re-reading runtime state.
